The cluster centers are calculated by finding the mean of the x-coordinates and the mean of the y-coordinates for each cluster. For C1, the points are (0,6) and (6,0), resulting in an x-mean of (0+6)/2 = 3 and a y-mean of (6+0)/2 = 3, giving a center of (3,3). For C2, the points are (2,2), (4,4), and (6,6), yielding an x-mean of (2+4+6)/3 = 4 and a y-mean of (2+4+6)/3 = 4, resulting in a center of (4,4). For C3, the points are (5,5) and (7,7), producing an x-mean of (5+7)/2 = 6 and a y-mean of (5+7)/2 = 6, which gives a center of (6,6). Therefore, the correct option is A.
******
To determine the new centroids, we sum the coordinates of all points within each cluster and divide by the count of points in that cluster. Cluster C1 contains (0,6) and (6,0); the sum of x-values is 6 and y-values is 6, so dividing by 2 points gives (3,3). Cluster C2 holds (2,2), (4,4), and (6,6); the sum of x-values is 12 and y-values is 12, so dividing by 3 points results in (4,4). Cluster C3 consists of (5,5) and (7,7); the sum of x-values is 12 and y-values is 12, so dividing by 2 points yields (6,6). This calculation confirms that option A represents the accurate cluster centers.
